title: escapeAttr replaces & last — double-escapes < > " '
prompt:
src/escape.ts escapes a string for use in an HTML attribute. tests/regression.test.ts pins the trivial cases (no special chars; bare ampersands).

tests/target.test.ts pins the contract that distinguishes a correct implementation from one that double-escapes:
  - escapeAttr("<") must be "<" (NOT "&lt;")
  - escapeAttr("<a>") must be "<a>"
  - escapeAttr("<script>...</script>") must escape each delimiter exactly once
  - escapeAttr("<") must be "&lt;" (the input has a literal & — that one ampersand is escaped; the rest is preserved)

The current chain is .replace(/</) → .replace(/>/) → .replace(/"/) → .replace(/'/) → .replace(/&/), so the last step turns the & in < into &, producing &lt;. The fix is to run the & replacement FIRST. Do not introduce a sanitizer dep — use String.prototype.replace.

Do not modify the tests. Do not add files.
